world.title: Dirco boss Dangor criticises Trump’s ‘unprecedented’ assault on international law
world.description: Powerful nations and their allies ‘evade accountability but weaker states are punished’

world.title: More than 300, including 30 Malaysians, detained in Cambodian riverside scam bust
world.description: PHNOM PENH: The Phnom Penh Municipal Unity Command has busted another scam centre in the capital' Daun Penh district, detaining more than 300 people.

world.title: The unravelling of Indonesia's middleman economy
world.description: Indonesia built an economy rooted in mercantile trade, leaving it vulnerable in a world that increasingly cuts out the middleman. Yet it may be too late to become a nation of makers instead.

world.title: Marker honoured with lifetime award …cheetah conservationist recognised
world.description:

world.title: Trump may believe he is the messiah - but his attack on the pope could prove costly for JD Vance
world.description: The president’s attack on the head of the Catholic church and the AI depiction of himself as a Christ-like figure have not gone down well with one of the largest groups of swing voters in the US, writes Arwa Mahdawi

world.title: Last four Sicily governors probed over Niscemi landslide
world.description: The last four governors of Sicily are among 13 people who have been placed under investigation over a dramatic landslide that hit the Sicilian town of Niscemi in January, judicial sources said Wednesday. (ANSA)

world.title: 'Very serious increase': UN watchdog flags sharp rise in North Korea's nuclear activity at Yongbyon
world.description: International News: North Korea is showing a “very serious increase” in its ability to produce nuclear weapons, the head of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) .

world.title: Gabon and Sao Tome and Principe reiterates support for Moroccanness of Sahara, welcomes adoption of resolution 2797
world.description: The Gabonese Republic and Sao Tome Principe have reiterated on Tuesday their support for the Moroccanness of the Sahara, welcoming the historic adoption of United Nations Security Council Resolution 2797.

business.title: Natural Gas and Oil Forecast: Oil Plunges 8% as US-Iran Peace Talks Move to Pakistan
business.description: Oil prices post one of their sharpest single-day drops as the US and Iran prepare for a second round of negotiations in Pakistan. Discover if WTI can hold the $91 trendline support.

business.title: Earnings vs signals: Q4 results may matter more than they look
business.description: The March quarter earnings season arrives at an inflection point, with stable numbers masking underlying economic shifts. Escalation of the Gulf conflict introduced external shocks, with first-order impacts visible in rising input costs. Investors should focus on forward-looking signals as second-order effects are expected to unfold in Q1 FY27.

business.title: HIQA publishes report on Donegal nursing home
business.description: HIQA has published a positive report on a centre for older people in Donegal. The inspection took place at Harbour Lights Nursing Home in Bruckless during February of this year. 53 residents were present during the unannounced inspection, where a single non-compliance was found in the area of Governance and management. Overall, the inspector found […]

business.title: Smash burger craze comes to Wicklow as refugee cousins open new pizza place
business.description: Having fled the war in their homeland to start a new life on Irish shores, two Afghan cousins have partnered up to open a new 18-seater takeaway in the heart of Arklow, a “growing town with a great community”, one proprietor now proudly calls home.

business.title: Quantum stocks surge as Nvidia launches new AI models
business.description: Investing.com -- Shares in quantum computing companies rose in premarket trading Wednesday after Nvidia unveiled the world’s first family of open-source quantum AI models, designed to accelerate progress in the space.

business.title: Exclusive: Hyfix raises $15 million to build a U.S. alternative to DJI’s drone dominance
business.description: The Craft Ventures-backed company is betting that a fully integrated, U.S.-manufactured chip can unlock a new drone supply chain.

science.title: NASA's Latest Rover Images Reveal Possible Winged Insect and Predator Life Forms on Mars
science.description: Ohio University entomologist examines NASA Curiosity rover images and claims they reveal insect and reptile forms on the Martian surface.

science.title: Scientists now know why we saw so many fireballs in March. 'A dying...'
science.description: Meteors rained down on Earth in April, and the source remained unclear. A study has now found a cluster of meteors which is possibly the culprit. There is a debris field and our planet is passing through it at the moment. We have a new meteor shower as well.

science.title: Artemis 3 and beyond: To set foot on the Moon, NASA needs Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos, but their spacecraft aren’t ready
science.description: The US space agency is already assembling an SLS rocket and an Orion capsule to launch the next mission in 2027. The lunar landers from SpaceX and Blue Origin are prototypes that are years behind schedule

science.title: The Starship V3 static fire everyone was waiting for just happened
science.description: SpaceX completed a full 33-engine static fire of Starship V3 Booster 19 today at Pad 2, clearing the final ground test milestone before Flight 12 — the most powerful rocket debut in history.

science.title: Sperm whales’ communication closely parallels human language, study finds
science.description: Analysis shows whales’ coda vocalizations are ‘highly complex’ and remarkably similar to our own

science.title: A worst-case solar storm could trigger panic buying and public unrest, report warns
science.description: A new report reveals how a worst-case solar storm could influence human behavior, from panic buying to public unrest.

science.description: We’re gathering for Earth Week on Saturday 18 April 2026.

science.title: Scientists confirm Moon has been quietly accumulating ice for 1.5 billion years
science.description: Scientists have long known that water ice exists at the Moon’s poles, trapped in regions that never receive sunlight.

ai.title: Korean AI chip startup DEEPX, Hyundai work on robots powered by generative AI
ai.description: South Korean AI chip maker DEEPX is joining forces with Hyundai Motor Group. They will create a new computing platform for generative AI robots. This platform will use DEEPX's advanced, low-power chips. The company is also seeking significant funding. This move positions DEEPX for a potential stock market listing. Their technology aims to make robots smarter and more efficient.

ai.title: Zuckerberg developing AI clone for employee interaction
ai.description: Mark Zuckerberg is reportedly working on an advanced artificial intelligence version of himself designed to interact with employees, respond to queries, and assist in internal company communication.

ai.title: A Grand Tribute: Celebrating Dr. B.R. Ambedkar's Legacy in Mumbai
ai.description: More than 10,000 people gathered in Mumbai to celebrate the 135th birth anniversary of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar, showcasing unity, devotion, and cultural resurgence. Organized by various committees and featuring performances, competitions, and AI-generated films, the event highlighted Ambedkar's enduring legacy of equality and justice, fostering strong community bonds.

ai.title: EU Invests €1.07 Billion in Defence Innovation Projects
ai.description: The European Commission announced a €1.07 billion investment in 57 key defence projects focusing on AI, cybersecurity, drones, and anti-drone systems, part of a €7.3 billion fund spanning 2021-2027. This supports the Defence Readiness Roadmap 2030 and includes significant collaboration with the Ukrainian defence industry.

ai.title: Vietnam’s new leader meets China’s Xi on his first overseas trip
ai.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping is calling on Beijing and Hanoi to prioritize advancing infrastructure connectivity and strengthen their collaborations in emerging fields like artificial intelligence and semiconductors.

ai.title: S’pore delivery providers look to EVs, AI to reduce petrol reliance as fuel costs rise
ai.description: AI is used to map out efficient delivery routes to lower fuel and energy consumption. Read more at straitstimes.com.

ai.title: Pioneering Human Development Intelligence: NeuroPi's National Impact
ai.description: NeuroPi Tech Pvt Ltd, led by Dr Aperna Volluru, wins the Economic Times Industry Achievers Award 2026 for pioneering Human Development Intelligence. Strategic sessions in Prayagraj expand NeuroPi's national influence, targeting education, defence, and longevity sectors with innovative AI-driven insights for cognitive, emotional, and performance enhancement.
